meta.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopen Date
meta.question: By what date will the Strait of Hormuz reopen to normal traffic (≥80% of 2024-2025 baseline transit volume, sustained for ≥30 consecutive days, per Lloyd's List Intelligence + EIA tanker data)?
meta.resolution: First 30-day window where AIS-confirmed transit volume reaches ≥80% of baseline (mean of 2024 + 2025 daily transits per Lloyd's/EIA)

priorJustifications.H1: Rapid resolution (<5mo from May 2026) is unlikely given current kinetic posture and absence of formal de-escalation negotiations.
priorJustifications.H2: Late 2026 / early 2027 reopening requires sustained de-escalation pressure (oil shock impact, third-party mediation); plausible but not modal.
priorJustifications.H3: Mid-2027 reopening reflects typical timeline of major maritime conflict resolution — modal scenario.
priorJustifications.H4: Extended closure beyond 2027 reflects tail risk of locked-in regional war or formal blockade regime.

meta.quantitativeAnchor: Quantitative anchor class: Lloyd's List war-risk premium term structure for Hormuz transits + oil futures backwardation curve. Retro-fit if those datapoints diverge >10% from drafted distribution.
redTeamGapsAddressed: Chokepoint reference class N=3 too narrow — missing Shatt al-Arab 1980-88 (closely analogous)
Post-Khamenei IRGC decision-making structure not characterized — Powell-analog empty
Iran 14-point proposal contents not extracted — H2 trajectory unscored
Israeli operational posture / war aims not covered
China leverage / strategic appetite for fast vs slow reopen unquantified
meta.priorRevisionRationale: 10-source corpus shows: (a) Active war ongoing despite April 8 conditional ceasefire — H1 (full reopen by Sep 2026) constrained by Israeli regime-change posture and US blockade. (b) Iran economic pressure (rial 1.81M, IMF -6.1% GDP, 68.9% inflation, food 105%) creates strong incentive for resolution within 6-12 months supporting H2/H3. (c) China+Pakistan mediation active; China economic pressure for reopen. (d) Iran 14-point plan offers realistic deal trajectory but Trump rejected; substantial give needed from both sides. (e) Reference class bimodal: Suez 1956 (5mo), Bab-el-Mandeb (24mo), vs Suez 1967 (8yr), Shatt al-Arab (8yr). Modal-prior closures resolved by political settlement. Net: H1 down 3pp (Israel/US blockade headwind), H3 up 5pp (more time helps if H2 deal slips), H4 down 2pp (Iran economic pressure makes 8-year-Shatt-analog less likely than otherwise).
meta.lensSetReason: Initial lens set during topic creation per skills/news-scan.md mapping

indicator_cleanup_history.summary: Added anti_h4_transit_recovery_toward_baseline to close H1/H2-direction observability gap per Plan B directional-symmetry fix. Indicator pairs with existing t1_transit_below_25pct_3mo on same metric (causal_event_id=hormuz_transit_band) and same fixture-data anchor.
Added t2_transit_recovery_70pct (shape-reviewed NOT_RESOLUTION x2) + observable blocks on diplomatic + mediation indicators. Closes H1/H2-direction observability gap.

Added t2_named_recovery_action_initiated + t2_preparatory_kinetic_or_blockade. Both shape-reviewed NOT_RESOLUTION x2. Closes news-flow-vocabulary gap surfaced by 2026-05-06 scan (Project Freedom, mine deployment, Iran-UAE attacks).

2 firings applied. t2_named_recovery_action_initiated multi-ref (ev_004+ev_001) to satisfy confidence_inflation; t2_preparatory_kinetic_or_blockade single-ref (decay path, n_firings=1 -> 2). 3 ev_ids cleared from flagged queue. 106 entries remain (recycled/data-tracking/rhetoric/operator-judgment/1 orphan ev_139). Envelope: cleanup-proposals/calibration-hormuz-reopen-2027__2dfb09130f.json. Posteriors: H1 0.0050->0.0050, H2 0.0076->0.0090, H3 0.6616->0.7693, H4 0.3258->0.2167.

warnings: No anti-indicators defined — every hypothesis needs at least one indicator that would REDUCE its probability

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — actions, not opinions
Each must have a clear fired/not-fired threshold
Every hypothesis needs at least one pro AND one anti indicator
Governor flags unfalsifiable hypotheses as DEGRADED

tier1_critical.desc: AIS-confirmed Hormuz transit reaches 40-55% of 2024-2025 baseline sustained 30 days in any window after May 2026 (precursor; resolution band starts at 80%, gap widened from prior 60% threshold). Source: Lloyd's List + EIA.
AIS-confirmed Hormuz transit at 40-65% sustained 30 days WITHIN 2026-10-01 to 2027-03-31 window only. Source: Lloyd's / EIA.
AIS-confirmed Hormuz transit at 40-75% sustained 30 days WITHIN 2027-04-01 to 2027-09-30 window only. Source: Lloyd's / EIA.
AIS-confirmed Hormuz transit stays below 25% of baseline for 3 consecutive months at any point in 2026-2027. Source: Lloyd's / EIA.
tier1_critical.note: Lloyd's List Intelligence — weekly Hormuz transits fall from 44 to 39 (week to May 3) — The resoluti

observable._note: Transit %, monthly avg from Lloyd's/EIA. Baseline=60% (uninformative midpoint between collapsed-near-0 and recovered-near-90%). At single-print 40% → ~57% strength toward H4.

tier1_critical.desc: Iran + Gulf-state OR US-led negotiation produces a public on-record framework agreement that EXPLICITLY references Strait of Hormuz transit guarantees / freedom-of-navigation clauses (no specific transit date — date-naming agreement would resolve, not update).

Direct kinetic strike on >=2 commercial vessels in Hormuz OR Iranian/US naval confrontation with confirmed casualties OR US on-record kinetic strike on Iranian territory OR Iran strike on US base with casualties. (Merged from prior t1_kinetic + anti_us_iran_strikes since these are one underlying event.)
tier1_critical.note: Iran says Strait of Hormuz passage to be ensured after US pauses operation; French container ship st

observable._note: Count of vessel strikes OR US/Iran naval-confrontation OR named-base strike events in 30-day window. 1 event = ~50% strength toward H4 (incidents weaken reopen). 2+ = full LR.

tier2_strong.desc: Lloyd's List war-risk premium for Hormuz transits drops >=50% from Q2 2026 peak, sustained 30 days. Source: Lloyd's List Intelligence. REV5: causal_event_id removed — paired indicator (reinsurer re-entry) is a distinct decision-making body and different timescale.

observable._note: % change from Q2 2026 peak. 0=at peak (uninformative), -50=full strength toward H1/H2. Negative direction = drop = stress easing.

tier2_strong.desc: Public confirmation (US State / Iranian MFA on-record statement) of resumed direct or back-channel diplomatic talks specifically addressing Hormuz. Source: State Dept / IRNA.

observable._note: Each on-record State/IRNA-confirmed Hormuz-specific diplomatic activity counts.

tier2_strong.desc: Confirmed active mediation by China, Oman, Qatar, OR EU3 producing on-record Iran-side concession statement. Source: AP / SPA / Xinhua / EU readouts.

observable._note: Each named mediation effort by China/Oman/Qatar/EU3 with on-record Iran-side concession counts.

tier2_strong.desc: Iran-Gulf or Iran-US announce a verbal ceasefire/de-escalation arrangement WITHOUT formal agreement text, persisting >=60 days but with documented incidents within the window (fragile, not a deal — supports H3 timeframe). Source: AP / Reuters / IRGC statements.
tier2_strong.note: Shipping in Strait of Hormuz at a standstill despite US-Iran ceasefire — Even post-ceasefire traffic

observable._note: Each named on-record fragile-ceasefire-holding-with-documented-incident report counts. Threshold=2 (matches news-flow proxy for 60-day persistence with incidents). Single observation = alpha=0.5 partial LR toward H3.

tier2_strong.desc: Major Western reinsurer (Lloyd's syndicate, Munich Re, Swiss Re) publicly resumes selective Hormuz transit underwriting at risk-loaded rates >=2x pre-crisis (signal of partial market normalization, supports H3). Source: Insurance Insider / Reactions. REV5: causal_event_id removed — distinct decision-making body from Lloyd's premium quotes.

AIS-confirmed Hormuz transit reaches >=70% of 2024-2025 baseline (significant recovery short of the 80% resolution threshold; observable provides continuous partial-LR for sub-70% recovery progress). Source: Lloyd's List + EIA. Direction-symmetric counterpart to t1_transit_below_25pct_3mo.

observable._note: Direction-symmetric counterpart to t1_transit_below_25pct_3mo. Threshold=70% (below 80% resolution). Baseline=5% (crisis floor) so any progress above ~5% provides partial-LR toward H1/H2. At transit=17% (Lloyd's recent), alpha=(17-5)/(70-5)=0.18 -> mild partial signal. Closes A15-style schema gap.

tier2_strong.note: The Week in Charts: Omani ships transit Strait of Hormuz eastbound; Tanker stocks bounce back amid w

tier2_strong.desc: Named US-led OR multilateral-coalition military/operational action specifically aimed at reopening Hormuz transit (e.g., 'Operation Project Freedom' launches, named US Navy escort operations, joint coalition announcements, transit-guarantee initiatives). Each named on-record action counts. Source: AP / Reuters / DOD / coalition statements.

observable._note: Each named US-led/multilateral recovery operation counts.

tier2_strong.note: Operation Project Freedom launch (canonical: ev_004 NPR/WFAE on-record) + first transits observed (ev_001 PBS). Multi-ref to satisfy confidence_inflation gate; single_observation indicator, first firing.

tier2_strong.desc: Iranian preparatory kinetic activity OR de-facto blockade reinforcement short of formal decree (e.g., mine deployment, IRGC fast-boat sortie surge, expansion of declared/de-facto control zones, Iran cross-border strikes on Gulf states straining the ceasefire). Each named on-record activity counts. Source: AP / Reuters / OSINT.

observable._note: Each preparatory-kinetic or blockade-reinforcement event counts.

tier2_strong.note: Iran expanded Hormuz control zone (Bloomberg). Single_observation indicator with prior n_firings=1; LR decay 0.65 applied.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Iran rial USD black-market rate depreciates >25% from Q2 2026 baseline sustained 60 days, OR Iranian crude exports drop below 1.0 mbpd sustained 90 days. Source: bonbast.com + Kpler.

anti_indicators.desc: Iranian government issues on-record decree/announcement formalizing closure or restricted-passage regime for Hormuz. Source: IRNA / Iranian MFA.

Iran rial APPRECIATES >=15% sustained 60 days OR Iranian crude exports recover to >=2.0 mbpd sustained 90 days (genuine anti-H4: economic relief reduces regime incentive to maintain closure). Source: bonbast.com + Kpler.

Announced bilateral framework agreement (per t1_bilateral_deal) is publicly repudiated, withdrawn, or violated within 60 days of signing OR negotiations explicitly break down per State/IRNA on-record statements (REV4: LR mass redistributed across H2/H3/H4 — collapsed deal more plausibly delays than implies indefinite closure). Source: State / IRNA / AP.

Verified IRGC fast-boat sortie rate drops >=50% from Q2 2026 peak sustained 60 days OR US Fifth Fleet announces visible escort tempo reduction / CSG redeployment out of Gulf region (kinetic posture relaxation independent of diplomatic/economic indicators). Source: CENTCOM + OSINT (Marine Traffic + Planet Labs).

observable._note: IRGC fast-boat sortie rate change from Q2 2026 peak. 0=at peak (uninformative). -50=full anti-H4 LR (kinetic posture relaxing).

anti_indicators.desc: AIS-confirmed Hormuz transit RECOVERS toward 2024-2025 baseline volume — observable as monthly transit % of baseline rising from crisis floor (~25%) toward resolution threshold (80%). Higher transit recovery directionally supports H1/H2 (faster reopen) and is anti-H4 (no reopen). Source: Lloyd's List + EIA tanker data. Companion to t1_transit_below_25pct_3mo (which catches the H4-direction signal on the same metric).

actorModel.description: Reopening of Strait of Hormuz to >=80% baseline transit hinges on: Iran regime calculus (economic pressure vs prestige), US-Iran kinetic posture, Gulf-state and third-party (China/Oman/EU) mediation, and insurance market willingness to underwrite transit. Lloyd's AIS data is the resolution observable.

iran_regime.name: Iran Regime (Khamenei + IRGC + MFA)
iran_regime.role: Sole party with closure authority. Domestic political constraints, regime survival, and IRGC institutional interests dominate.
iran_regime.decisionStyle: Slow-moving, ideology-anchored on US/Israel; willing to accept severe economic pain to avoid public concession.
biases: IRGC institutional incentive to maintain crisis
filters: Sanctions enforcement, oil export volumes, rial value
overrides: Domestic protest wave or succession crisis forces accelerated compromise OR doubling down

us_admin.name: US Administration (Trump + DoD + State)
us_admin.role: Combination of military posture (Fifth Fleet escort tempo, CSG positioning) and diplomatic pressure shapes Iran's options.
us_admin.decisionStyle: Willing to threaten/use kinetic force; transactional on diplomatic deals; unpredictable timing.
biases: Tension between Pentagon caution and admin desire for action
overrides: Major US casualty event forces hard escalation regardless of de-escalation track

gulf_states.name: Gulf States (KSA, UAE, Qatar, Oman)
gulf_states.role: Direct economic exposure. Mediation conduits (Oman, Qatar) and sometimes party to deal frameworks (KSA on regional integration).
gulf_states.decisionStyle: Pragmatic, hedging between US alignment and Iran de-confliction.
biases: UAE/KSA prefer permanent solution; Qatar hedges
filters: Oil prices, regional shipping costs, US security guarantees
overrides: Direct Iranian strike on Gulf infrastructure forces alignment shift

china_mediator.name: China (mediator + economic buyer)
china_mediator.role: Largest buyer of Iranian crude. 2023 Saudi-Iran rapprochement broker. Has incentives + leverage to mediate.
china_mediator.decisionStyle: Behind-the-scenes; prefers structural settlements that lock in oil supply.
biases: Will not openly confront Iran despite pressure
filters: Iranian crude flows, Chinese strategic petroleum reserve
overrides: US tariff escalation against China complicates mediation incentive

insurance_market.name: Lloyd's of London + Major Reinsurers
insurance_market.role: Sets cost-of-transit. Reinsurer re-entry signals market verdict on durability of any de-escalation.
insurance_market.decisionStyle: Risk-loaded pricing; conservative on partial settlements.
biases: Slow to reduce premiums after de-escalation
filters: Vessel incident reports, AIS transit data, naval posture

ais_lloyds_data.name: Lloyd's List Intelligence + EIA Tanker Tracking
ais_lloyds_data.role: Resolution observable. AIS-confirmed >=80% baseline sustained 30 days resolves.
ais_lloyds_data.decisionStyle: Methodical; some lag on real-time transit attribution.
filters: AIS signal data, vessel manifests, satellite confirmation

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — Iranian MFA statements are RHETORIC (24h TTL); on-record decree is EVENT; AIS data is DATA.

DIPLOMATIC TRACK COUPLING — Bilateral deal + war-premium drop + diplomatic channel can fire simultaneously on one negotiation cycle. REV4 declares causal_event_id linking; require independent observable confirmation per H2/H3 progression.
INSURANCE LAG — Lloyd's premium reduction lags actual de-escalation by 30-90 days. Don't treat insurance signal as leading indicator.
KINETIC SYMMETRY — H4 (no reopen by 2027) requires sustained kinetic conflict OR formal blockade decree. Singular incidents do not push H4 strongly without escalation pattern.
DEAL COLLAPSE NON-LINEAR — REV4: deal collapse redirects to H2/H3 (delay), not directly to H4 (indefinite closure). Most documented framework collapses produce 6-18 month delays not abandonment.

lloyds_ais_transit.note: Resolution observable: 80% baseline sustained 30 days. 4 transit-band indicators share per_event_member shape + causal_event_id=hormuz_transit_band.

evidenceLog.text: May 7: CENTCOM confirmed three US destroyers (Truxtun DDG-103, Rafael Peralta DDG-115, Mason DDG-87) came under coordinated Iranian missile, drone and small-boat attack while transiting Strait of Hormuz. US intercepted all threats, no US assets struck. US responded with self-defense strikes on Iranian missile/drone launch sites, C2 facilities, and ISR nodes. Iran claimed significant damage to US vessels; CENTCOM denied any hits. Trump called it a love tap and threatened harder response.
evidenceLog.source: CENTCOM statement + CNBC + GCaptain + CBS News (cross-verified)
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Naval kinetic engagement under ceasefire. Both sides exchanged fire. No confirmed US casualties. US struck Iranian military targets in response.

evidenceLog.text: May 7: French shipping giant CMA CGM confirmed Malta-flagged containership San Antonio was struck while transiting Strait of Hormuz, injuring multiple crew. IMO reported 8 seafarers wounded, 32nd reported shipping incident since conflict began. Another CMA CGM vessel (Saigon) exited Gulf via Oman coastline route.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Civilian vessel strike confirms ongoing kinetic risk to commercial shipping.

evidenceLog.text: May 10-11: Qatari LNG tanker Al Kharaitiyat crossed Hormuz for first time since war began, reportedly approved by Iran to build confidence with Qatar and Pakistan. Second Qatari LNG tanker (Mihzem) also transiting toward Pakistan. Government-to-government deal with Iran approving passage via Iranian-controlled northern route.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: First structured transit mechanism since war. Narrow, negotiated channel only. Not general reopening.

evidenceLog.text: May 12: Senior IRGC officer stated Iran redefined Strait of Hormuz from narrow area to strategic zone stretching from Jask (east) to Siri Island (west). Iranian lawmakers drafting legislation to formalize management with clauses forbidding passage to hostile-state vessels.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Qualitative escalation: Iran formalizing and expanding control zone. Institutionalization of blockade regime.

evidenceLog.text: May 12: UK and France co-hosted virtual meeting of 40+ defense ministers to plan multinational security mission for Hormuz. France deployed aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle, UK sent HMS Dragon to pre-position. Iran deputy FM warned foreign warship deployment would meet decisive response.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: First concrete step toward international security architecture. Pre-positioning naval assets.

evidenceLog.text: May 12: Chinese FM Wang Yi called Pakistani Deputy PM Ishaq Dar, urging Pakistan to intensify mediation between Iran and US for Hormuz stability. Timing coordinated with Trump-Xi summit May 14-15 in Beijing.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: China-Pakistan mediation intensification ahead of Trump-Xi summit.

evidenceLog.text: May 10-11: Trump rejected Iran counterproposal as totally unacceptable. Iran demanded war reparations, full sovereignty over Hormuz, end of sanctions, release of seized assets. Trump described ceasefire on massive life support with 1 percent chance of living. Oil jumped to over 105 dollar per barrel.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Diplomatic deadlock deepens. Both sides maximalist positions. No convergence visible.

evidenceLog.text: May 11: Aramco CEO stated global oil markets losing 100 million barrels weekly while Hormuz shut. Windward identified only 9 commercial tanker transits through Hormuz on May 11 vs 100+ daily baseline. IMO estimates 2000 vessels trapped near Hormuz.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Transit at less than 10 percent baseline. 100M barrel weekly loss. Humanitarian impact: 45M at hunger risk per UN.

evidenceLog.text: May 11: South Korea announced initial investigation findings that two unidentified objects struck the HMM NAMU, a South Korean-operated vessel, while it was anchored in the Strait of Hormuz about one minute apart, causing explosion and fire. Investigation ongoing to determine responsibility. Expected to give Seoul grounds to reconsider joining US-led Hormuz security missions.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Confirmed kinetic strike on allied vessel. Expands geographic scope of attacks to include anchored ships, not just transiting ones. May push Seoul toward US-led coalition.

evidenceLog.text: May 12: Maersk confirmed it has halted all Strait of Hormuz transit operations amid escalating tensions following the US-Iran exchange of fire. This follows Maersk's successful transit on May 4 where the US-flagged Alliance Fairfax exited under Project Freedom US military escort. Major carrier unwilling to commit to regular transits given unresolved security environment and Iranian attacks on non-compliant vessels.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Even after a protected passage, major carrier withdraws. Signals commercial confidence is near zero. BIMCO warned Project Freedom pause complicates risk calculus.

evidenceLog.text: May 11: Somali piracy resurgence — three ships hijacked in three weeks (Honour 25, Eureka oil tankers, cargo ship Sward) as international naval patrols diverted by Hormuz and Red Sea crises. Institute for Security Studies and Danish Institute for Strategy and War Studies report organized crime groups in Puntland well-resourced using large dhows as mother ships. 2011 crisis cost ~7B annually; this round adds ~1M dollar per voyage fuel cost for Africa-rerouting ships already displaced by Hormuz closure.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Compounding maritime security crisis. Naval asset diversion creates secondary disruption layer. Not directly Hormuz but a consequence of Hormuz closure — naval assets pulled from Western Indian Ocean.

evidenceLog.text: May 10: Iranian parliament lawmakers drafting legislation to formalize management of Strait of Hormuz with clauses explicitly forbidding passage to vessels from hostile-state nations. Iran parliament claims transit fees for strait would generate 15B dollar annually, roughly a third of current oil revenue.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Institutionalization of blockade via domestic legislation. Transit fee proposal suggests Iran seeking revenue replacement model. Makes reopening a legislative process, not just military one.

evidenceLog.text: May 11: Commercial shipping through Hormuz operating under dark/EMCON conditions per IRGC tightens grip report. IRGC fast craft activity expanded across both Hormuz corridors deploying swarm-style formations near commercial traffic. Only dark fleet-linked LPG vessels among few transiting. IRGC tightening operational control rather than loosening.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Qualitative tightening of IRGC enforcement. EMCON conditions indicate commercial sector operating under combat-level precautions. Swarm formations suggest active anti-access strategy.

evidenceLog.text: May 10-11: Iran deputy FM warned against planned French-British maritime mission in strait threatening decisive and immediate response. Iran military declared full readiness to protect nuclear sites where 440+ kg of 60-percent-enriched uranium is stored. Drone attacks targeted Gulf Arab nations over weekend: UAE shot down two drones, Qatar reported drone ignited fire on ship off coast, Kuwait airspace entered by drones.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
evidenceLog.note: Regional escalation beyond Hormuz. Gulf states directly targeted. Iran linking nuclear site protection to maritime threats. Weekend drone campaign shows sustained capacity for multi-theater operations.

evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE — flagged for indicator review (no matching indicator at scan time)
